title: Parosela nutans
taxonomy: Parosela nutans belongs to the family Annonaceae, commonly known as the custard apple family. This plant is native to tropical regions of Central and South America. It is a type of evergreen shrub or small tree that can grow up to several meters tall. The classification of Parosela nutans is further divided into the genus Parosela, which is a part of the tribe Dendrochaete. The specific classification of this plant is still being studied and updated in various botanical sources.

intro: Parosela nutans, also known as the "Brazilian angelica," is a plant species within the family Apiaceae, characterized by its aromatic leaves and stems. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections and digestive issues.
